demographics.living_situation: lives at home with spouse who is the primary carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My knee is locked in absolute agony — it feels like bone grinding on bone, and I cannot even put my foot on the floor. I can't get to the bathroom.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain in the right knee suddenly got much worse over the last day or two after a long walk and now she can't stand on it at all — the spouse rang because she's stuck in bed.

symptom_narrative.onset: the right knee has ached for years but flared badly over the past 48 hours after an unusually long walk
symptom_narrative.duration: chronic joint pain for years; this severe flare for about 2 days
symptom_narrative.character: deep, dull, grinding ache with a grating/crackling feeling on moving the joint
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has ever been — she is now stuck in bed and cannot weight-bear
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: mainly the right knee; also long-standing aching in both knees and the right hip
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worse with any movement or standing, eased by resting; short morning stiffness that wears off within about half an hour
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: resting and keeping the leg still
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any weight-bearing, standing or trying to walk
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: a grinding/crackling in the joint on movement; the spouse has noticed she has been limping to keep weight off the leg
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: long-standing chronic aching in the weight-bearing joints, never this severe; no prior episodes of a hot, red, suddenly swollen joint
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: acutely bedbound tonight; her normal baseline is walking with a stick

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: known long-standing arthritis in both knees and the right hip
past_medical_history.how_managed: regular paracetamol and a walking stick at baseline

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a distant history of mild gastritis / stomach upset
past_medical_history.how_managed: avoids anti-inflammatory tablets because of it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives at home with her spouse, who is the primary carer; the bathroom is down a long hallway and there is no bedside commode or frame at home tonight

communication_style.typical_message_style: the distressed spouse relays most of the history and is asking for strong pain relief and help getting her to the toilet safely

objective_data_offered.value: spouse says she cannot put any weight on the right leg and is stuck in bed; she has been limping to keep weight off it for the last little while
spouse says she has not had any fevers, shivering or shaking chills, and does not feel hot to touch
right knee looks a little swollen; she is grimacing and guarding the leg and will not let it be moved
bathroom is down a long hallway; no bedside commode or walking frame available at home
